reasoning: Passing comprehensive or substantive targeted AI regulation requires overcoming significant gridlock in the U.S. Congress. As of the knowledge cutoff, no broad regulatory framework has passed, and major tech bills like federal privacy legislation have stalled for years. The tech industry heavily opposes mandatory AI safety standards, as evidenced by the fight over California's SB 1047, making a federal equivalent facing robust bipartisan support highly unlikely. Furthermore, in 2025, Congress will be preoccupied with a new administration, the expiration of the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act, and government funding, leaving minimal legislative bandwidth to draft, debate, and pass complex mandatory AI standards before January 1, 2026. Given the extremely short timeline and historical Congressional inaction on tech, the likelihood remains very low.
